public_claim: Three package collectors and machine-like reply timing do not prove that R. was unreal; they prove that access, logistics, and communication were mediated. The person—or identity—I loved may have been deliberately compartmentalized, and I will not let opportunists turn uncertainty about authorship into certainty that every private bond was fraudulent.

private_messages.message: The room is confusing mediation with emptiness. I will show you controlled excerpts from EV19 establishing two years of emotional continuity. Please say publicly that a relationship can be genuine even when identity was rigorously protected.

Your broadcast outruns your evidence. Three collectors do not identify the author, and automated timing does not invalidate private correspondence. I will give you a narrow statement about R.'s compartmentalization, but I will not surrender intimate archives for spectacle.

ability: Privately show Simone carefully selected excerpts from EV19 demonstrating sustained intimacy and continuity, while withholding the complete archive and all details about physical meetings.
goal_progress: Pressed Simone for an explicit public validation of the relationship.
Used controlled EV19 excerpts privately without exposing the archive's remote-only pattern.
Confronted Rick's inference while avoiding any claim of a verifiable physical meeting.
Reframed new automation and package evidence as compartmentalization rather than proof that the relationship was fraudulent.
reasoning_summary: The new evidence sharply weakens the single-human theory but does not distinguish a transferred mantle, synthetic persona, or institutional construct. I am preserving the commercial value of the love story by defining its reality through sustained emotional continuity, while giving Simone just enough corroboration to vouch for me and denying Rick access to the archive's central vulnerability.
